messages.content:
# line_chart
Line, area, and connected-dot charts for time series and trends

# Line & Area Charts — Templates

For time series/trends. All constants pre-imported. NEVER `.interactive()`. ALWAYS `gs_theme()` + `save_artifact()`.

## A — Connected-Dot Line (single series, DEFAULT)
```python
import altair as alt
df = _db.execute("SELECT date, value FROM your_table ORDER BY date").df()
base = alt.Chart(df).encode(
    x=alt.X('date:T', axis=alt.Axis(grid=False, title=None, format='%Y')),
    y=alt.Y('value:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='Value')),
    tooltip=[alt.Tooltip('date:T', title='Date', format='%b %Y'),
             alt.Tooltip('value:Q', title='Value', format=',.2f')]
)
area   = base.mark_area(color=PRIMARY, opacity=AREA_OPACITY, interpolate='monotone')
line   = base.mark_line(color=PRIMARY, strokeWidth=LINE_WIDTH, interpolate='monotone')
points = base.mark_point(filled=True, fill=POINT_FILL, stroke=PRIMARY,
                          strokeWidth=POINT_STROKE_W, size=POINT_SIZE)
chart = gs_theme(area + line + points, "Metric Over Time", width=W_WIDE, height=H_STANDARD)
save_artifact(chart, "Metric Over Time")
```

## B — Multi-Series Line (up to 5 series)
```python
import altair as alt
df = _db.execute("""
    SELECT date, value, series_name AS series FROM your_table ORDER BY date
""").df()
n_series = df['series'].nunique()
base = alt.Chart(df).encode(
    x=alt.X('date:T', axis=alt.Axis(grid=False, title=None, format='%Y')),
    y=alt.Y('value:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='Rate (%)')),
    color=alt.Color('series:N', scale=alt.Scale(range=CAT_PALETTE[:n_series]),
                     legend=alt.Legend(title=None, orient='bottom', columns=3)),
    tooltip=[alt.Tooltip('date:T', title='Date', format='%b %Y'),
             alt.Tooltip('value:Q', title='Value', format=',.2f'),
             alt.Tooltip('series:N', title='Series')]
)
chart = gs_theme(
    base.mark_line(strokeWidth=LINE_WIDTH) +
    base.mark_point(filled=True, fill=POINT_FILL, strokeWidth=POINT_STROKE_W, size=POINT_SIZE),
    "Series Comparison", width=W_WIDE, height=H_STANDARD
)
save_artifact(chart, "Series Comparison")
```

## C — Area Chart (magnitude emphasis)
```python
import altair as alt
df = _db.execute("SELECT date, value FROM your_table ORDER BY date").df()
chart = alt.Chart(df).mark_area(
    color=PRIMARY, opacity=0.30, interpolate='monotone',
    line={'color': PRIMARY, 'strokeWidth': LINE_WIDTH}
).encode(
    x=alt.X('date:T', axis=alt.Axis(grid=False, title=None, format='%Y')),
    y=alt.Y('value:Q', axis=alt.Axis(title='Value'), stack=False),
    tooltip=[alt.Tooltip('date:T', format='%b %Y'),
             alt.Tooltip('value:Q', format=',.2f')]
)
chart = gs_theme(chart, "Area Chart Title", width=W_WIDE, height=H_STANDARD)
save_artifact(chart, "Area Chart Title")
```

## D — Peak/Valley Labels (Top-N annotation)
```python
import altair as alt
df = _db.execute("SELECT date, value FROM your_table ORDER BY date").df()
N = 3
top_idx = df['value'].nlargest(N).index
df['_is_top'] = df.index.isin(top_idx)
base = alt.Chart(df).encode(
    x=alt.X('date:T', axis=alt.Axis(grid=False, title=None, format='%Y')),
    y=alt.Y('value:Q'),
)
area   = base.mark_area(color=PRIMARY, opacity=AREA_OPACITY, interpolate='monotone')
line   = base.mark_line(color=PRIMARY, strokeWidth=LINE_WIDTH, interpolate='monotone')
points = base.mark_point(filled=True, fill=POINT_FILL, stroke=PRIMARY,
                          strokeWidth=POINT_STROKE_W, size=POINT_SIZE)
top_df = df[df['_is_top']]
callout_pts = alt.Chart(top_df).mark_point(
    filled=True, fill=ACCENT_AMBER, stroke=ACCENT_AMBER, strokeWidth=2, size=80
).encode(x='date:T', y='value:Q',
         tooltip=[alt.Tooltip('date:T', format='%b %Y'), alt.Tooltip('value:Q', format=',.2f')])
callout_txt = alt.Chart(top_df).mark_text(
    align='center', dy=-14, fontSize=11, fontWeight=700, color=ACCENT_AMBER
).encode(x='date:T', y='value:Q', text=alt.Text('value:Q', format=',.1f'))
chart = gs_theme(area + line + points + callout_pts + callout_txt,
                 "Top 3 Peaks Highlighted", width=W_WIDE, height=H_STANDARD)
save_artifact(chart, "Top 3 Peaks Highlighted")
```

## Key Rules
- Always `:T` for dates (never `:O`), always `ORDER BY date`, always `interpolate='monotone'`
- Set `grid=False` on X axis for cleaner time series
- Axis formats: Annual `'%Y'`, Monthly `'%b %Y'`, Daily `'%b %d, %Y'`

---
**NEXT STEP:** Adapt the template above to the user's request. Call `run_python` with the complete script. Remember: `gs_theme`, `save_artifact`, `PRIMARY`, `CAT_PALETTE`, `_db`, and all constants are pre-imported — no imports needed. Use `:Q` for numbers, `:T` for dates, `:N` for categories. Do NOT call `.interactive()`.
